summary: The spec table is well-researched and source code claims are accurate (line numbers verified: ast_to_hir.rs:1421, symbols.rs:202, parse_brace_expr:226-291). However two critical gaps will cause compile-time build failures: (1) `types/check_expr.rs` has an exhaustive match on `Expr` (lines 11-346) with NO catch-all — adding `Expr::Compare` to the AST will immediately break compilation; its spec `types/type-checker.md` is absent from both the reference table and spec plan. (2) `resolve/pass.rs` has an exhaustive match on `Expr` in `resolve_expr` (lines 280-400) with NO catch-all — same breakage; `resolve/name-resolution.md` is also absent. Additionally, the spec plan entry `dict-unpack-and-chained-cmp-lowering` bundles two separate issues (#831 and #973) in a single spec file, violating the one-logical-unit rule.

findings.description: types/check_expr.rs lines 11-346 contains an exhaustive match on `&expr.node` covering every `Expr` variant with NO catch-all `_` arm (the match closes at line 346 after `Expr::UnpackTarget`). Adding `Expr::Compare` to the `Expr` enum in parser/ast.rs will immediately produce a Rust compile error: 'non-exhaustive patterns: `Expr::Compare` not covered'. The spec `crates/cclab-mamba/types/type-checker.md` (which lists check_expr.rs as a covered file and notes it 'infers types for... comparisons') is completely absent from the reference table and spec plan. At minimum, a match arm `Expr::Compare { left, ops, comparators } => self.tcx.bool()` is required, and a spec plan entry must add this requirement.

resolve/pass.rs lines 280-400 contains an exhaustive match on `&expr.node` in `resolve_expr` with NO catch-all `_` arm (ends with `Expr::IntLit | ... | Expr::Ellipsis => {}` at line 399 then closes). Adding `Expr::Compare` without a new arm here will produce a compile error. `resolve_expr` must recurse into the Compare node's left, ops comparators to resolve any name references. The spec `crates/cclab-mamba/resolve/name-resolution.md` (which covers resolve/pass.rs) is absent from both the reference table and spec plan.

The spec plan entry `dict-unpack-and-chained-cmp-lowering` modifies `crates/cclab-mamba/lower/ast-to-hir.md` and explicitly addresses both dict unpack desugaring (#831) and chained comparison desugaring (#973). These are two separate issue features with distinct data structures (Expr::DictLit vs Expr::Compare), separate test concerns, and separate acceptance criteria. The checklist rule 'each spec file covers exactly one logical unit (not multiple unrelated concerns)' is violated. They should be split into: (a) `dict-unpack-lowering` modifying ast-to-hir.md with sections for #831 fix, and (b) `chained-cmp-lowering` modifying ast-to-hir.md with sections for #973 desugaring. Alternatively, merge_strategy could be used but both sets of changes should have clearly named sections.

findings.location: reference_context.md — hir/hir.md key requirements, unresolved uncertainty
findings.description: The key requirements for `crates/cclab-mamba/hir/hir.md` state: 'HirExpr::Compare in HIR (line 146 listed in hir.md R1) may already exist for simple comparisons; needs verification'. This uncertainty is explicitly flagged but not resolved. The actual spec R1 unambiguously lists 'Compare' as a HirExpr variant ('Literal, Name, BinOp, UnaryOp, BoolOp, Compare -- basic expressions'). This is not a minor ambiguity — knowing whether HirExpr::Compare exists determines the desugaring strategy (desugar AST Compare → HIR Compare vs. desugar AST Compare → HIR BoolOp). The reference context should have resolved this before proposing the spec plan.

findings.location: reference_context.md — key requirements cite source line numbers, not spec IDs
findings.description: Several entries (ast-to-hir.md, hir-to-mir.md) cite source code line numbers (e.g., 'ast_to_hir.rs:1421-1439', 'hir_to_mir.rs:3801-3816', 'symbols.rs line 202') rather than spec requirement IDs. While these line numbers are accurate (verified: line 1421 confirmed, line 202 confirmed exactly), they are implementation-level details. The key requirements column should cite spec R-IDs (e.g., 'R5: mb_dict_merge') and note gaps where no spec requirement exists yet, rather than mixing code pointers with spec references.

Both correctly identified as high relevance. expressions.md R1 (Pratt parser, comparison operators at precedence 7-8) confirmed accurate. ast.md R1 (BinOp, DictLit, no Compare variant) confirmed accurate. The proposed new Expr::Compare and CmpOp structures are well-motivated and consistent with CPython 3.12 semantics.

Correctly identified R5 mb_dict_merge as the relevant requirement. Confirmed: symbols.rs line 202 registers mb_dict_merge exactly as described. Confirmed: mb_dict_merge is registered but no lowering path calls it (gap for #831). R5 spec text matches the key requirements exactly.
Correctly confirms no lexer changes needed. DoubleStar token (** operator) confirmed in R1. All comparison tokens confirmed. Low relevance score is appropriate.

Correctly notes that the existing P1 spec covers R1-R5 (#847, #845, #846, #835, #848) and that #973 and #831 are not covered there. This is accurate — neither issue number appears in the spec. Low relevance is appropriate for background context.

findings.location: source code verification — parse_brace_expr and ast_to_hir DictLit
findings.description: parse_brace_expr DoubleStar handling at expr_compound.rs lines 226-291 confirmed accurate. ast_to_hir.rs DictLit lowering at lines 1421-1439 confirmed with exact NoneLit sentinel comment text matching the reference context description. These source-level observations are reliable.

revisions_required: Add `crates/cclab-mamba/types/type-checker.md` to the reference table (high relevance) with key requirement: check_expr.rs lines 11-346 has exhaustive Expr match with no catch-all — adding Expr::Compare requires a new arm; add a spec plan entry (modify) for this spec with sections: requirements, changes

Add `crates/cclab-mamba/resolve/name-resolution.md` to the reference table (high relevance) with key requirement: resolve/pass.rs resolve_expr lines 280-400 has exhaustive Expr match with no catch-all — adding Expr::Compare requires recursion into left/comparators; add a spec plan entry (modify) with sections: requirements, changes

Split `dict-unpack-and-chained-cmp-lowering` spec plan entry into two separate entries: `dict-unpack-lowering` (covers #831 DictLit NoneLit sentinel fix → dict_new+dict_merge desugaring) and `chained-cmp-lowering` (covers #973 Expr::Compare → BoolOp desugaring with temp vars), both modifying `crates/cclab-mamba/lower/ast-to-hir.md` with distinct sections

Resolve the HirExpr::Compare uncertainty in hir.md key requirements — the spec clearly lists Compare in R1; update the key requirements to state definitively that HirExpr::Compare exists and clarify whether the desugaring plan should target HirExpr::Compare directly or lower to BoolOp(And, pairwise BinOps)
